svg.title: Lesson 7 insurance fraud detection multi-agent architecture
svg.desc: Five-agent system showing EnhancedOrchestrator coordinating ClaimProcessorAgent, CustomerServiceAgent, MedicalReviewAgent, and FraudDetectionAgent, with tools, RAG knowledge base, and in-memory database

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does a claim request contain?')
sendPrompt('What does the EnhancedOrchestrator do in Lesson 7?')
g.text: Inherits ComplaintResolutionOrchestrator · coordinates all agents · routes fraud checks

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What privacy levels exist in the system?')
sendPrompt('What does ClaimProcessorAgent do in Lesson 7?')
sendPrompt('What does CustomerServiceAgent do in Lesson 7?')
sendPrompt('What does MedicalReviewAgent do in Lesson 7?')
sendPrompt('What does FraudDetectionAgent do in Lesson 7?')
sendPrompt('How does FraudPatternDetector work with TF-IDF?')
sendPrompt('What fraud patterns are in the RAG knowledge base?')
g.text: FP-001 Rapid claim · FP-002 Upcoding · FP-003 Duplicate · FP-004 Phantom · FP-005 Frequency · FP-006 Date · FP-007 Provider shopping
TfidfVectorizer converts patterns to vectors · cosine_similarity ranks matches

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What data does the in-memory database store in Lesson 7?')
g.text: patients · claims · complaints — populated by DataGenerator · resets on restart

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does AccessControl restrict data in Lesson 7?')

svg.text: 2. ClaimProcessorAgent processes claim via process_new_claim → stored in database
3. FraudDetectionAgent runs check_claim_for_fraud → TF-IDF vectorizes claim → cosine similarity vs 7 fraud patterns
4. Risk score (low/medium/high/critical) + recommendation returned to orchestrator → final response
